system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        "(k|epsilon)"   1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

RAS

mixture

    }

    transport

boundaryField

    }

    outlet

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   0.001;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet section (straight duct along x-axis)
    ( -50  25  25 )   // 0
    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 1
    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 2
    ( -50 -25  25 )   // 3

    (   0  25  25 )   // 4
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 5
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 6
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    (  25   0  25 )   // 8
    (  25   0 -25 )   // 9
    (  25 -50 -25 )   // 10
    (  25 -50  25 )   // 11

    // Block 2 - transition section
    (  25  -75  25 )   // 12
    (  25  -75 -25 )   // 13
    (  25 -125 -25 )   // 14
    (  25 -125  25 )   // 15

    // Block 3 - long outlet section
    ( -500  -75  25 )   // 16
    ( -500  -75 -25 )   // 17
    ( -500 -125 -25 )   // 18
    ( -500 -125  25 )   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    // Vertices: bottom face (0 1 5 4), top face (3 7 6 2)
    // Viewed from +y, bottom face (0 1 5 4) goes +x -> +x -> -z -> -x
    // Reversed to CW: 0->4 (+x) ->5 (-z) ->1 (-x) gives CCW for exterior (+y normal needed)
    // Correct CW for exterior (+y normal): 0->1 (-x) ->5 (-z) ->4 (+x)
    hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    hex (7 11 3 9 6 10 2 8) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Transition section
    hex (11 10 15 9 14 13 2 12) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: Outlet straight section
    hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 14) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 17 18 19)
        );
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 5 1)
            (4 8 9 5)
            (8 11 10 9)
            (11 12 13 10)
            (12 16 17 13)
        );
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
            (3 2 14 15)
            (15 19 18 14)
        );
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)
            (7 11 3 8)
            (3 15 12 11)
            (15 19 16 12)
        );
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 5 6 2)
            (5 9 10 6)
            (2 10 13 14)
            (14 13 17 18)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (11 10 15 9 14 13 2 12) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(11 10 13 14) 

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run3/runs/squareBend/1/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 57.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block 11 10 15 9 14 13 2 12 has inward-pointing faces

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (11 10 15 9 14 13 2 12) ... has inward-pointing faces
    4(11 10 13 14)
```

**Diagnosis:**

The error clearly identifies that the face `(11 10 13 14)` of Block 2 is inward-pointing. Let me trace through the geometry:

Vertices involved:
- 11: `(25, -50, 25)`, 10: `(25, -50, -25)`, 13: `(25, -75, -25)`, 14: `(25, -125, -25)`

These are ALL at x=25 — they lie on a plane perpendicular to the x-axis. The face `(11 10 13 14)` connects:
- 11→10: `(0, 0, -50)` (along -z)
- 10→14: `(0, -50, 0)` (along -y)
- 14→13: `(0, 0, 50)` (along +z)
- 13→10: `(0, 50, 0)` (along +y)

Applying the right-hand rule: fingers from 11→10 (-z), curl toward 10→14 (-y) — thumb points in the **+x direction**. But for Block 2, this face is an **internal face** shared with Block 1. The block interior should be on the **negative-x side** (since Block 2 extends from x=25 toward x=-∞ in the negative x-direction). The normal pointing **+x** means the interior is on the wrong side — hence "inward-pointing."

**Fix:** Reverse the winding order of Block 2. Change:
```
hex (11 10 15 9 14 13 2 12)
```
to:
```
hex (11 9 15 10 14 13 12 2)
```

This reverses both faces so the block interior lies on the correct side (negative-x).

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cascading error

Since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh was generated. This resolves automatically once blockMesh succeeds.

## Summary of Changes

Only **Block 2** in `blockMeshDict` needs to be changed:
- Change `hex (11 10 15 9 14 13 2 12)` to `hex (11 9 15 10 14 13 12 2)`

This reverses the vertex ordering so that the internal-facing bottom/top faces have normals pointing toward the block interior (negative-x direction), making all blocks geometrically valid. All other configuration files (controlDict, fvSchemes, fvSolution, physicalProperties, momentumTransport, and all 0/ field files) should remain unchanged.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Change Block 2 hex definition from hex (11 10 15 9 14 13 2 12) to hex (11 9 15 10 14 13 12 2) to fix inward-pointing faces')]
